data.title: VCX Stock Rockets 2,300% Above Assets, Fueling Worries over Octane Trading Frenzy
data.description:
Shares of Fundrise Growth Tech Fund (VCX) surged to a record $575 on Wednesday, trading at roughly 30 times its net asset value (NAV) of $18.97 per share. While the stock settled at $380 by the close, it jumped another 21% early Thursday to $457.99, putting the stock at a 2,314% premium to its NAV.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

VCX Price Soars over 1,300% in One Week

This comes even as questions have started to swirl about the risks of such a massive price bid. The closed-end fund went public on the New York Stock Exchange a week ago at $31.25, meaning that its price has risen by more than 1,360% over the last seven days, as of early Thursday.

The surge is being driven by retail investors pursuing early pre-IPO (initial public offering) exposure to Wall Street's top AI and tech heavyweights such as SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Databricks. VCX holds stakes in these highly sought-after private companies, offering indirect exposure as typical venture capital rounds are inaccessible to regular investors.

To Jump or Not to Jump In?

However, market observers have pointed out that such an investment may not be worth it at current price levels for long-horizon investors. Jack Shannon, equity strategies principal at Morningstar, has described jumping in at this level for the long haul as "a horrible investment."

Yet, not all observers are worried, as some see the surge as driven by organic interest, with the fund offering more direct exposure to private companies like OpenAI and Anthropic than can be gotten via large-cap tech leaders such as Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Nvidia (NVDA). This distinctive positioning of VCX could mean more upsides ahead.

Nonetheless, if sentiment sours in the AI, software, and data-infrastructure names that dominate the fund, the speculation-driven gains could quickly unwind. 

Is VCX a Good Buy?

TipRanks' technical indicators suggest that VCX is a Buy. This is based on six Bearish, one Neutral, and nine Bullish signals logged over the past week.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: U.S. Bill to Ban Chinese Robots Could Boost Tesla and Domestic AI Stocks
data.description:
U.S. lawmakers are taking aim at a new front in the tech race. A bipartisan group in the Senate plans to introduce a bill that would ban federal agencies from buying or using robots made by Chinese firms.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

The proposed law, called the American Security Robotics Act, is backed by Senator Tom Cotton and Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer. It would also block federal funds from being used in any way tied to these systems. In addition, a companion bill is expected in the House of Representatives, which shows broad support across both parties.

Security Risks Drive the Push

Lawmakers say the move is tied to rising concerns over data and control. These robots, which can move and operate in real-world settings, may collect sensitive data through cameras and sensors. As Senator Cotton put it, "Robots made by Communist China threaten Arkansans' privacy and our national security."

Similarly, Schumer warned that Chinese firms "are running their standard playbook… trying to flood the U.S. market with their technology, which presents real security risks." At the same time, the bill allows limited use for research by the military and law enforcement groups. However, those systems must not send or receive data from China.

Competition Heats Up in Robotics

Meanwhile, the bill comes as the race in humanoid robots picks up pace. Chinese firms such as Unitree and Agibot are gaining ground and are preparing to list shares in China this year. On the U.S. side, Tesla (TSLA) is among the key players working on humanoid robots that could be used in factories or homes.

As a result, the policy move also reflects a broader push to support U.S. tech in a fast-growing field. Robots are seen as a key part of the next wave of AI, with uses that range from factory work to daily tasks.

For investors, the trend is clear. The U.S. is starting to treat robotics in the same way it has treated chips and telecom in recent years, with tighter rules on foreign tech and a focus on local growth.

Overall, this bill signals that robotics is moving into the center of the tech and policy debate, with clear ties to both national security and future market leadership.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all the notable publicly traded companies in the Robotics sector side-by-side. This is a great tool to evaluate each stock and the Robotics sector as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Will SoundHound AI Stock (SOUN) Short Squeeze after All This Insider Selling?
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) investors are on edge today, as the stock sits near its 52-week low. The big question in the market is whether a "short squeeze" could suddenly send the price back up, or if the recent phase of insider selling is a warning sign that more trouble is ahead.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Recent Insider Selling Supports Investor Caution

Lately, the top bosses at SoundHound have been selling a lot of stock. This week, several key leaders, including CEO Keyvan Mohajer, the CFO, and the COO, disclosed sales of thousands of shares. For example, on March 20, the CEO sold over 124,000 shares at an average price of about $6.79.

While seeing insiders sell can be scary, it's important to know why they are doing it. Much of this selling was actually done to cover tax bills from their stock bonuses. However, the timing is tough. The stock has dropped nearly 19% in the last week alone, and seeing the leaders sell while the price is at a one-year low of $6.36 makes many people nervous about the company's near-term health.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Will Rivian's Deal With Uber, New R2 Model Jumpstart the Stock?
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN), an Irvine, California-based electric vehicle manufacturer, has struck a $1.25 billion deal with rideshare kingpin Uber (UBER) . The objective is to put 50,000 autonomous Rivian R2 vehicles on Uber's ride-sharing platform by 2031.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Will the Uber deal give Rivian shareholders the jumpstart they've been looking for?

A Downhill Ride

Rivian shareholders have had a rough ride. Rivian went public in November of 2021 at about $100 per share, and quickly rose to $172.

Since then, it's been a downhill ride. The stock now trades in the mid-teens, a decline of over 90%. In addition, Rivian shares have fallen by 19% since the start of this year.

Rivian saw a decline in sales last year to just 42,247 units, from 51,579 units in 2024. That decrease occurred as global EV sales climbed by an estimated 20% from 2024 to 2025.

In Need of a Jumpstart

Rivian needs a catalyst to jumpstart sales and regain favor. The company is hoping the R2, a mid-size electric SUV, will be that catalyst.

The vehicle, projected to cost from $45,000, is expected to begin shipping next month. Rivian is accepting orders with just a $100 deposit.

Rivian says it received 68,000 orders for the R2 on its first day of availability — well above the automaker's total sales in 2025. Orders for the R2 are now believed to exceed 200,000.

The R2 is also the key to Uber's deal with Rivian. The two companies plan to introduce autonomous R2's to Uber's platform in 2028, rising to 50,000 R2's by 2031.

The Missing Ingredient

The news sounds amazing on the surface. It's a huge deal to put 50,000 autonomous R2's on Uber's rideshare platform, and over 200,000 R2 reservations from the general public.

If the news is truly great, why aren't institutions buying Rivian?
data.author: TheStreet Pro

data.title: VCX Fund Is Up 1,900% — But Here Are 3 Risks Every Investor Should Know First
data.description:
The Fundrise Innovation Fund (VCX) has been making waves since its NYSE debut on March 19, 2026. Starting with a net asset value (NAV) of $18.97, the stock recently closed at $380.97, marking a staggering 1,900% premium over its initial NAV. Behind VCX's meteoric rise lie risks such as market volatility and liquidity challenges that could affect returns. Here's what every investor needs to know before considering VCX for their portfolio.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Is VCX?

VCX is a NYSE-listed public venture fund giving investors access to high-growth private tech companies. It invests across all stages—from early to late-stage startups—and holds positions through potential IPOs. Top holdings include Anthropic (~21%), Databricks (~18%), OpenAI (~10%), Anduril (~7%), and SpaceX (~5%). The fund charges an annual management fee of 1.85%.

Let's take a look at 3 major risks.

Valuation and Premium Risk

VCX trades at a huge premium to its NAV, signaling that much of its recent surge is driven by speculative demand rather than fundamental value. The fund is heavily concentrated in AI, software, and data infrastructure and if AI and tech sectors stumble or face regulatory hurdles, VCX's performance could be sharply impacted. Meanwhile, these underlying holdings aren't liquid; VCX can't quickly sell them to meet market demand. This can cause the fund's price to swing dramatically, especially if many investors try to sell at once.

Jack Shannon, equity strategies principal at Morningstar, said, "With the implied valuations when you have this premium, your upside is gone." He added that VCX t might attract hype and heavy short-term trading, but for long-term investors, it's a very risky bet at today's price.

While VCX offers retail investors access to high-growth private tech companies, its extreme price action makes it a high-risk, high-fee vehicle.

The Lockup Risk

Lockup risk refers to the period during which a fund cannot sell its private company holdings. VCX has a 6-month lockup for early investors, creating a potential supply overhang. When the lockup ends, a wave of selling could push the price down. While this isn't a flaw in the fund itself, it's a structural risk that could cap gains or trigger a pullback once locked-in shares become available.

In short, VCX's lockups make it less flexible and more volatile than traditional ETFs that hold fully liquid public stocks.

Competition Risk

VCX's huge premium exists because it's one of the few ways for retail investors to access private tech giants like OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX. But that scarcity won't last. When any of these companies go public—or a competing fund launches—VCX's exclusivity disappears. More supply means the premium could drop.

Is VCX a Buy?

According to TipRanks' technical analysis, VCX fund is a Buy, based on nine Bullish, six Bearish and one Neutral signal over the past week.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Chainlink Emerges as Critical Infrastructure for Tokenized Assets
data.description:
Chainlink (LINK-USD) is emerging as a critical infrastructure for tokenized assets, serving as a trusted data bridge to address the blockchain oracle problem. The challenge has always been simple to describe and hard to solve: smart contracts cannot access real-world data on their own. Every price feed, every settlement trigger, every compliance check requires a bridge like Chainlink, and the demand for it is accelerating. What started as a niche tool for decentralized finance (DeFi) developers has quietly become the standard infrastructure for some of the world's largest financial institutions.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Oracle Market Is Chainlink's to Lose

The numbers on market share are hard to argue with. As of October 2025, Chainlink secured $100 billion in value across DeFi markets and commands approximately 70% oracle market share by value, according to Messari. On Ethereum (ETH-USD) specifically, that share climbs even higher.

This dominance did not happen by accident. Chainlink runs over 2,000 price feeds across major chains. It powers liquidation thresholds for Aave (AAVE-USD), Synthetix (SNX-USD), and GMX. It handles collateral valuation for the largest lending markets in DeFi. Switching costs are high, integrations are deep, and alternatives are years behind in institutional trust.

Wall Street Is Paying Attention

The institutional validation has moved well beyond pilot programs. JPMorgan's (JPM) Kinexys platform completed a cross-chain Delivery versus Payment transaction with Ondo Finance (ONDO-USD) in 2025, using Chainlink Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) as the secure orchestration layer between a permissioned interbank network and a public blockchain, according to JPMorgan. The transaction was a cross-chain test, but one conducted by the blockchain division of one of the world's largest banks using live institutional payment rails. That distinction matters.
data.author: Hillary Remy

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/26/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Edge Lower on Middle East Uncertainty
data.description:
Stock futures trended lower early Thursday on uncertainty over the resolution of the U.S.-Iran conflict. Investors continue to monitor developments in the Middle East after Iran ruled out direct talks with the U.S. despite senior officials in Tehran reportedly reviewing a proposal to end the war. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.76%, 0.63%, and 0.51%, respectively, at 5:22 a.m. EST on March 26.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

After yesterday's decline, oil prices moved higher this morning due to continued geopolitical tensions. At the time of writing, the international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 3.27% to $105.56 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) increased 3.33% to trade at $93.33 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes ended higher on hopes of a ceasefire. The Dow Jones and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.66% and 0.77%, respectively, while the S&P 500 gained 0.54%.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield inched higher to 4.37%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price hovered around $4,477 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower as hopes of a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran faded.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower

Asia-Pacific markets fell on Thursday as Iran indicated that it is not interested in holding direct talks with the U.S. to end the war.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 1.89%. In China, the Shanghai Composite was down 1.09%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.46%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei closed lower by 0.27%, while the Topix declined 0.22%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: CrowdStrike (CRWD) Expands Strategic Collaborations with Intel, IBM amid AI Push
data.description:
Cybersecurity company CrowdStrike (CRWD) announced expanded partnerships with tech giant IBM (IBM) and chipmaker Intel (INTC) to ensure safety amid growing adoption of artificial intelligence (AI). The company extended its multi-year collaboration with Intel to optimize its Falcon platform for Intel-powered AI PCs. Meanwhile, CrowdStrike expanded its strategic collaboration with IBM to accelerate agentic SOC (Security Operations Center) transformation.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade IBM with leverage
  • New trading tool for IBM bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade IBM

CRWD stock has declined 18% so far in 2026 due to macro headwinds and concerns that generative AI tools could disrupt traditional software companies. Concerns about AI disruption increased after Anthropic unveiled its Claude Code Security.

CrowdStrike Expands Partnership with Intel

Under the extended partnership with Intel, CrowdStrike's Falcon platform will offer high-performance security at enterprise scale by leveraging Intel Threat Detection Technology and Intel vPro's hardware-assisted recovery.

By combining CRWD's Falcon platform with Intel's AI PC architectures, the two companies intend to ensure real-time security and data protection and enhance their co-engineering pipeline to further boost AI-powered security at the edge.

CRWD Extends Deal with IBM

Meanwhile, CrowdStrike's expanded partnership with IBM involves integrating the cybersecurity company's Charlotte AI platform with IBM's Autonomous Threat Operations Machine (ATOM), the tech giant's autonomous SOC orchestration engine, to enable machine-speed investigation and containment.

The deal will also integrate the Falcon platform into IBM Consulting's managed Threat Detection and Response services and global X-Force Cyber Range to offer cyber crisis simulations and help enterprises prepare for emerging threats.

Overall, the partnership combines the SOC, IBM ATOM, and Charlotte AI to assess detections across endpoint, identity, and cloud environments.

Is CRWD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

With 27 Buys and nine Holds, CrowdStrike scores a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average CRWD stock price target of $484.33 indicates 25.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: META Stock Slides after Losing Landmark Addiction Case—DA Davidson Calls It a 'Setback'
data.description:
Shares of Meta Platforms (META) were down by 0.47% in after-hours trading on Wednesday after the company lost a landmark U.S. lawsuit over social media addiction. A Los Angeles jury found Meta and Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google negligent for designing platforms that harm young users. D.A. Davidson's four-star-rated tech analyst Gil Luria called the verdict a "setback" for these tech giants.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Luria added that the verdict could stretch through future cases and appeals, but it may eventually push these companies to add consumer protections, which could slow growth. Luria has a Buy rating on META stock with a price target of $850, implying an upside of over 40% from current levels.

Backdrop to the Case

The case involves Kaley, a 20-year-old who became addicted to Instagram and YouTube as a minor due to features like infinite scroll. The jury found both companies "negligent" in app design and failing to warn users of potential harms.

This marks Meta's second major loss this week. On March 24, a New Mexico jury found the company guilty of hiding information about child sexual exploitation risks and the harmful effects of its platforms on children's mental health. The jury fined Meta $375 million for violating safety law.

Other defendants, TikTok and Snap (SNAP), settled on confidential terms before the six-week trial began.

Meta Liable for $4.2M in Damages

A Los Angeles jury ordered Meta to pay $4.2 million and Google $1.8 million. While these amounts are small for tech giants spending over $100 billion annually, the real impact could be much larger.

However, the real significance isn't just the money. The ruling could set a legal precedent, opening the door for hundreds of similar lawsuits, forcing changes to app designs, and increasing regulatory and investor pressure. In other words, the long-term effects could be far more costly than the initial payout.

Is META Stock a Good Buy Now?

While the verdict may affect short-term momentum, the long-term outlook for Meta remains stable. Turning to Wall Street, Meta's shares have a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks, based on 40 Buys and five Holds assigned over the last three months. Furthermore, the average META price target of $865.58 indicates a 45.5% growth potential from its current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak
